segments.text: The boy Samuel ministered before the Lord under Eli. In those days the word of the Lord was rare; there were not many visions.
One night Eli, whose eyes were becoming so weak that he could barely see, was lying down in his usual place.
The lamp of God had not yet gone out, and Samuel was lying down in the house of the Lord, where the ark of God was.
Then the Lord called Samuel.
Here I am.
segments.speaker: Samuel

segments.text: And he ran to Eli and said,
Here I am; you called me.
segments.speaker: Samuel

segments.text: But Eli said,
I did not call; go back and lie down.
segments.speaker: Eli

segments.text: So he went and lay down.
Again the Lord called,
Samuel!
segments.speaker: The LORD

segments.text: And Samuel got up and went to Eli and said,
Here I am; you called me.
segments.speaker: Samuel

segments.text: “My son,” Eli said,
I did not call; go back and lie down.
segments.speaker: Eli

segments.text: Now Samuel did not yet know the Lord: The word of the Lord had not yet been revealed to him.
A third time the Lord called,
Samuel!
segments.speaker: The LORD

segments.text: And Samuel got up and went to Eli and said,
Here I am; you called me.
segments.speaker: Samuel

segments.text: Then Eli realized that the Lord was calling the boy.
So Eli told Samuel,
Go and lie down, and if he calls you, say,
segments.speaker: Eli

segments.text: Speak, Lord, for your servant is listening.
segments.speaker: Samuel

segments.text: So Samuel went and lay down in his place.
The Lord came and stood there, calling as at the other times,
Samuel! Samuel!
segments.speaker: The LORD

segments.text: Then Samuel said,
Speak, for your servant is listening.
segments.speaker: Samuel

segments.text: And the Lord said to Samuel:
See, I am about to do something in Israel that will make the ears of everyone who hears about it tingle.
segments.speaker: The LORD

segments.text: At that time I will carry out against Eli everything I spoke against his family—from beginning to end.
segments.speaker: The LORD

segments.text: For I told him that I would judge his family forever because of the sin he knew about; his sons blasphemed God, and he failed to restrain them.
segments.speaker: The LORD

segments.text: Therefore I swore to the house of Eli, ‘The guilt of Eli’s house will never be atoned for by sacrifice or offering.’
segments.speaker: The LORD

segments.text: Samuel lay down until morning and then opened the doors of the house of the Lord. He was afraid to tell Eli the vision,
but Eli called him and said,
Samuel, my son.
segments.speaker: Eli

segments.text: Samuel answered,
Here I am.
segments.speaker: Samuel

segments.text: What was it he said to you?
segments.speaker: Eli

segments.text: Eli asked.
Do not hide it from me. May God deal with you, be it ever so severely, if you hide from me anything he told you.
segments.speaker: Eli

segments.text: So Samuel told him everything, hiding nothing from him. Then Eli said,
He is the Lord; let him do what is good in his eyes.
segments.speaker: Eli

segments.text: The Lord was with Samuel as he grew up, and he let none of Samuel’s words fall to the ground.
And all Israel from Dan to Beersheba recognized that Samuel was attested as a prophet of the Lord.
The Lord continued to appear at Shiloh, and there he revealed himself to Samuel through his word.
